blockingIssues.description: The discover scanner only inspects immediate children of each configured root, so default roots like `~/Documents` cannot discover nested repositories under organizer folders such as the `~/Documents/GitHubMe/minimax-skills` path called out by VAL-DISC-003.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement recursive traversal (or recursive fallback) so the scan can find matching `.*/skills` directories below the first level and group results by the actual project directory.

blockingIssues.description: The discover cache only INSERT OR IGNOREs newly found rows and never removes stale discovered_skills entries, so deleted or renamed project skills continue to appear in cached startup results after rescans.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Reconcile discovered_skills for the scanned roots on each run by deleting rows that were not rediscovered and updating metadata for rows whose paths/frontmatter changed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the scrutiny validator/reviewer procedure so Task-spawned `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` subagents can run with automatic execution approval in this worker environment, or document the manual-review fallback explicitly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In this discover-scan scrutiny run, both reviewer Task invocations (`complete-discover-backend` and `complete-discover-frontend`) exited immediately with `Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed`, so the validator had to review the transcript skeletons, handoffs, commit diffs, and current source manually.
